message:
refactor(tantivy_*): use RecorderStorage _with_cx siblings (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on to ticks 74/75. Wires the tick-74 RecorderStorage
trait _with_cx default methods into the Cx-first paths added
in ticks 52/53/54 (tantivy_ingest) and ticks 55/56/57/58
(tantivy_reindex). Each storage call inside a `*_with_cx`
method now routes through the Cx-first trait sibling so
backends with deeper cx threading (e.g. a future
timeout_with_cx-aware commit_checkpoint) see the cancellation
signal.

tantivy_ingest.rs changes (all inside _with_cx methods; legacy
paths untouched):

  * `IncrementalIndexer::run_with_cx` (tick 53): swaps
    `storage.read_checkpoint()` → `read_checkpoint_with_cx(cx,
    ...)` and `storage.commit_checkpoint()` →
    `commit_checkpoint_with_cx(cx, ...)` inside the per-batch
    loop.

  * `IncrementalIndexer::run_with_reader_with_cx` (tick 52):
    same two swaps — the reader-backed variant now has its
    checkpoint I/O cx-aware too.

tantivy_reindex.rs changes (all inside _with_cx methods; legacy
paths untouched):

  * `ReindexPipeline::full_reindex_with_cx` (tick 55): both
    read_checkpoint calls (the clear-before-start guard AND
    the resume lookup) now use `read_checkpoint_with_cx(cx,
    ...)`.

  * `ReindexPipeline::backfill_with_cx` (tick 57): read_checkpoint
    → `read_checkpoint_with_cx(cx, ...)`.

  * `ReindexPipeline::reindex_range_observed_with_cx` (tick 58):
    the per-batch commit_checkpoint inside the observed loop
    now uses `commit_checkpoint_with_cx(cx, ...)` — the
    observer callback immediately after gets to see cx-aware
    commit latency semantics.

  * `ReindexPipeline::index_loop_with_cx` (tick 55) and
    `index_loop_exclusive_with_cx` (tick 56): both inner loops'
    per-batch commit_checkpoint call now cx-aware. These
    helpers back `full_reindex_with_cx`, `reindex_range_with_cx`,
    and `backfill_with_cx`, so the refactor flows transitively
    into all their callers.

Edit methodology note: the two `index_loop_with_cx` /
`index_loop_exclusive_with_cx` commit_checkpoint blocks are
byte-identical locally (same indentation, same CheckpointConsumer
ordering). The Edit tool can't disambiguate them, so a small
Python one-liner replaced the two specific lines by
1-indexed line number. Legacy methods at lines 802, 1313, 1539
are untouched — verified by file inspection and by all legacy
tests still passing.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * tantivy_ingest:: — 123 passed / 0 failed (unchanged from
    tick 54)
  * tantivy_reindex:: — 94 passed / 0 failed (unchanged from
    tick 58)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (36.40s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

The refactor is pure wiring — no new public API, no behavior
change on the default trait impl path (default _with_cx
methods delegate to non-cx after a checkpoint). Concrete
backends that override trait _with_cx methods (none yet, but
the seam is now in place) will see per-call cancellation
without further refactoring in the consumer code.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
